calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement And Order
Off Calendar Nov-20-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SHAUNA N. CORREIA OF THE WEINTRAUB/TOBIN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-772-9655 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JOHN JENNINGS OF THE SHULMAN HODGES LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-949-340-3400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF MARCUS PARTIN'S Motion To Enforce Settlement And Court Order is denied. First, the Texas court denied Mr. Partin's non conveniens motion even though he expressly argued that Mr. Bennett/entities were attempting to forum shop and undermine the California rulings. Second, the Amended Petition makes it clear that the entities are seeking a declaration regarding the application of Texas law. That issue is distinct from what was litigated in this case. Third, a court will enjoin a parallel action only in extreme circumstances. "A parallel action in a different state presents sovereignty concerns that compel California courts to use judicial restraint when determining whether they may properly issue a TRO against parties pursuing an action in a foreign jurisdiction." Advanced Bionics Corp. v. Medtronic, Inc. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 697, 707. Here, such extenuating circumstances are not present. Lastly, it appears that Mr. Partin can raise the release that the parties agreed to on the record as a defense to the Texas action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

Off Calendar Nov-20-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SHAUNA N. CORREIA OF THE WEINTRAUB/TOBIN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-772-9655 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JOHN JENNINGS OF THE SHULMAN HODGES LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-949-340-3400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF MARCUS PARTIN'S Motion For Sanctions - The court denies the motion for sanctions. Plaintiff's motion seeks sanctions solely against Mr. Bennett for filing the Texas action. Mr. Bennett, however, is not a named party to the Texas action. The limited partnerships filed the action and any sanctions for the frivolous filing should be directed towards the limited partnership. A partnership is a collection of individuals. See Bedolla v. Logan & Frazer (1975) 52 Cal.App.3d 118, 127. There is no authority that allows this court to sanction merely one of the individuals that is part of the collection of individuals that compose the partnership. Plaintiff's remedy may be to bring a derivative action against Mr. Bennett if he is misusing the partnership. See Wallner v. Parry Professional Building, Ltd (1994) 22 Cal.App.4th 1446 (allowing limited partner to file derivative action where the general partner was accused of self-dealing). Moreover, there is nothing that precludes plaintiff from attempting to seek sanctions in Texas for the filing. Indeed, that court is better situated to determine what amount of sanctions is appropriate given that it is the court adjudicating the case. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
